event: Lamentation over the Death of Saint Francis
symbols: friars gathered around the body — the community Francis founded mourning its founder
monastery courtyard — the institutional legacy of Francis's radical poverty
heavenly vision above — the soul of Francis ascending to God
striped bier — the deathbed of a saint, a place where earth meets heaven
description: A horizontal panel painting showing the body of a tonsured friar in a brown habit lying on a striped bier in the center of a monastery courtyard. Numerous brown-robed Franciscan friars stand and kneel around the body in attitudes of grief and reverence. On the left, figures in white and red ecclesiastical vestments observe the scene. A small heavenly vision appears in the sky above. The courtyard is enclosed by simple monastery buildings with a bell tower visible on the right. The painting has a gold-leaf sky and characteristic early Renaissance spatial organization.
